name: Malicious Use of Control Panel
notes:
This rule detects malicious use of control panel items.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Kyaw Min Thein, Furkan Caliskan (@caliskanfurkan_)
SIGMA Title: Control Panel Items
SIGMA Description: Detects the malicious use of a control panel item


Confidence: MEDIUM

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success

when the event matches ( ( LOWER("Process Full Full Name") == 'reg.exe' OR LOWER("Filename") == 'reg.exe' ) AND ( LOWER("Command") LIKE '%add%' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '%currentversion\control panel\cpls%' ) ) OR ( ( LOWER("Command") LIKE '%.cpl' ) AND ( LOWER("Command") NOT LIKE '%\system32\%' AND LOWER("Command") NOT MATCHES '.*?(%system32%).*?' ) AND ( LOWER("Command") NOT LIKE '%regsvr32%' AND LOWER("Command") NOT LIKE '% /s %' AND LOWER("Command") NOT LIKE '%igfxcpl.cpl%' ) ) AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Malicious Use of Control Panel on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success

AND when the event matches ( 	( 		LOWER("Process Full Full Name") == 'reg.exe' OR LOWER("Filename") == 'reg.exe' 	) 	AND  	( 		LOWER("Command") LIKE '%add%' 		AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '%currentversion\control panel\cpls%' 	) ) OR ( 	( 		LOWER("Command") LIKE '%.cpl' 	) 	AND  	( 		LOWER("Command") NOT LIKE '%\system32\%' 		AND LOWER("Command") NOT MATCHES '.*?(%system32%).*?' 	) 	AND  	( 		LOWER("Command") NOT LIKE '%regsvr32%' 		AND LOWER("Command") NOT LIKE '% /s %' 		AND LOWER("Command") NOT LIKE '%igfxcpl.cpl%' 	) ) AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Malicious Use of Control Panel
Event description: Detects malicious use of control panel items.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
